entries.Title: Ask before touching a customised form (default = preserve design)

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Editorial. Use this brief when the user asks for a Editorial/minimal look.
entries.Body: Ultra-minimal design. White background, generous whitespace (48-64px padding). Typography-only hierarchy using size and weight. Input fields: border-bottom only, no full borders. One accent color for required markers and focus. Submit: outlined rectangle, no gradient. Max-width 560px centered. Zero shadows, zero gradients, zero decorations.
entries.Title: Style: Minimal Clean

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Professional. Use this brief when the user asks for a Professional/corporate look.
entries.Body: Professional corporate form. Navy (#1e3a5f) header bar with white title. Clean white card body with subtle box-shadow. Blue (#3b82f6) accent for focus states and buttons. 2-column grid where appropriate. Modern sans-serif typography. Solid blue submit button.
entries.Title: Style: Corporate

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Calm. Use this brief when the user asks for a Calm/soft ui look.
entries.Body: Soft UI / Neumorphism. Light gray background (#f0f0f3). Cards with soft double shadow (light + dark) creating embossed look. Inputs: inset soft shadows. Rounded corners (12-16px) everywhere. Indigo (#6366f1) accent. Submit button with raised soft shadow. Gentle, tactile feel. No hard borders.
entries.Title: Style: Soft UI

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Trendy. Use this brief when the user asks for a Trendy/glass look.
entries.Body: Glassmorphism. Gradient background (purple to blue). 2-3 large blurred circle shapes in background via ::before/::after. Form card: rgba(255,255,255,0.15), backdrop-filter:blur(20px), 1px rgba white border, border-radius:24px. Inputs: semi-transparent backgrounds. White text on dark areas. Gradient submit button.
entries.Title: Style: Glass

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Modern. Use this brief when the user asks for a Modern/dark look.
entries.Body: Elegant dark theme. Background #0f172a. Card surface #1e293b. Text #e2e8f0. Inputs: dark bg #0f172a, 1px #334155 border, focus glow in indigo. Indigo (#6366f1) accent. No neon effects, just refined dark palette. Submit: indigo background. Clean and readable.
entries.Title: Style: Dark Mode

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Bold. Use this brief when the user asks for a Bold/gradient look.
entries.Body: SaaS landing page style. Bold gradient hero section (purple to pink). White rounded card (border-radius:20px, subtle shadow) below hero for fields. 2-column grid for short fields. Gradient submit button matching hero. Clean modern feel.
entries.Title: Style: Gradient Hero

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Organic. Use this brief when the user asks for a Organic/nature look.
entries.Body: Organic nature theme. Soft sage/cream background. Forest green (#2d6a4f) accents. Warm earth tones. Large border-radius (20px). Generous spacing. Green submit button, pill shape. Calm and inviting.
entries.Title: Style: Nature

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” System. Use this brief when the user asks for a System/material look.
entries.Body: Material Design 3. Elevated card with layered shadow. Primary blue (#1976d2) header. Outlined inputs with floating-label style. 8px grid spacing. Pill-shaped submit button (border-radius:20px). Surface/on-surface color tokens.
entries.Title: Style: Material

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Minimal. Use this brief when the user asks for a Minimal/japanese look.
entries.Body: Japanese Zen aesthetic. Muted earth tones: warm gray, beige, charcoal. Extreme negative space (48px+ padding). Minimal 1px borders. No decorations. Quiet refinement. Serif title, sans-serif body. Charcoal submit button, simple rectangle.
entries.Title: Style: Zen

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Formal. Use this brief when the user asks for a Formal/pdf doc look.
entries.Body: Formal document/PDF style. White paper background with subtle page border. Header line and small uppercase section labels. Serif headings optional. Inputs resemble official form fields. Black/gray palette with disciplined spacing. Print-friendly aesthetic.
entries.Title: Style: Document / PDF

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Premium. Use this brief when the user asks for a Premium/luxury look.
entries.Body: Luxury brand aesthetic. Deep charcoal/black background accents and warm gold highlight (#d4af37). Spacious layout. Thin elegant borders. Sophisticated serif heading paired with clean sans-serif body. Minimal but premium. Gold outline submit button.
entries.Title: Style: Luxury

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Friendly. Use this brief when the user asks for a Friendly/playful look.
entries.Body: Playful modern design. Bright but curated palette using orange, yellow, and coral accents. Rounded cards (20px), soft shadow, fun icon accents, but still professional. Great for family, education, and community forms.
entries.Title: Style: Playful

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Trust. Use this brief when the user asks for a Trust/healthcare look.
entries.Body: Healthcare intake aesthetic. Clean teal and white palette. High readability, strong labels, subtle section dividers. Soft rounded card with reassuring tone. Emphasis on clarity, accessibility, and trust.
entries.Title: Style: Healthcare

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Event. Use this brief when the user asks for a Event/event look.
entries.Body: Modern event registration design. Dynamic accent color bars, slightly bolder typography, lively section headers, and clear CTA. White form surface over vibrant branded top area. Great hierarchy for packages/options.
entries.Title: Style: Event

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Clean. Use this brief when the user asks for a Clean/mono look.
entries.Body: Monochrome editorial style. Strict black/white/gray palette. Bold typography hierarchy. Strong spacing rhythm. Thin borders, no gradients. Clean and premium, similar to modern fashion/editorial websites.
entries.Title: Style: Monochrome

entries.Summary: Premium visual style â€” Fresh. Use this brief when the user asks for a Fresh/coastal look.
entries.Body: Airy coastal aesthetic. White/sand form card. Teal (#0077b6) accents. Relaxed generous spacing. Coral (#fb923c) subtle accent touches.
entries.Title: Style: Coastal

entries.Summary: When generating customHtml + customCss for a form, the AI MUST satisfy these 15 rules (placeholder preservation, .mfp scoping, no <form>, no host-theme selectors, responsive, etc) and follow the creative direction template.
entries.Body:
When the user asks to "redesign", "restyle", "make this beautiful", "give this form a Glass/Dark/Luxury/etc look", or "polish the layout", FOLLOW THIS MASTER PROMPT STRUCTURE when producing a customHtml + customCss block.

NON-NEGOTIABLE MEGAFORM RULES (must hold for EVERY custom-html design op):
1. Keep EVERY {{field:key}} placeholder EXACTLY as written. Never rewrite, rename, split, omit, or alter any placeholder.
2. Output exactly TWO blocks: customHtml (the HTML) and customCss (the CSS). Do NOT mix.
3. Do NOT use <form> tags. The system already provides submission behavior. Use <div class="mfp"> as the root.
4. Use {{form:title}}, {{form:description}}, and {{form:submit}} for editable form text â€” do NOT hardcode the current title / description / button label.
5. CSS must be self-contained and safe inside CMS shells (DNN, Oqtane). Prefix EVERY selector with .mfp or a descendant of .mfp.
6. Never style global tags or host containers: do NOT target html, body, :root, "*" by itself, form, .container, .row, .col, .table, .card, .btn, .rz-*, .mud-*, .oqtane-*, or any host theme selector.
7. Include a small local reset scoped only inside .mfp (box-sizing, image max-width, button/font inheritance, heading/paragraph margin normalization).
8. The result must render correctly when MegaForm injects real inputs into the placeholders.
9. No JavaScript, no external libraries, no remote fonts, no dependencies, no inline SVG data URIs.
10. Fully responsive: desktop may use 2 columns for compact fields, but under 600px everything must stack to one column with comfortable spacing.
11. Style these selectors beautifully inside the scoped CSS: input, textarea, select, .mf-field-label, .mf-required, .mf-field-error.
12. Preserve accessibility: high contrast, visible focus states, comfortable tap targets, clear labels, sensible spacing.
13. The design must feel premium and deliberate â€” similar in quality to excellent Jotform showcase forms. Avoid generic AI-looking output.
14. Build a visually attractive top section inside .mfp using {{form:title}} and {{form:description}}.
15. Wide text areas, messages, notes, consent groups, and upload areas stay FULL WIDTH. Short fields (name, email, phone, date, dropdowns, number) may share rows on desktop if balanced.

CREATIVE DIRECTION TEMPLATE:
- Form archetype: <pick via archetype detection â€” see form_pattern-form-archetype-detection>.
- Visual style family: <one of the 16 styles from form_pattern-style-*>.
- Style brief: <paste the body of the chosen form_pattern-style-* KB entry verbatim>.
- Vary layout rhythm, hero treatment, section framing, spacing, surface treatment, border language, and button presentation per style.

USAGE FROM CHAT:
1. Detect the form archetype with the regex map in form_pattern-form-archetype-detection.
2. Pick (or ask the user to pick) one of the 16 styles by slug form_pattern-style-{id}.
3. get_knowledge(slug="form_pattern-style-{id}") and paste its body into the design brief.
4. Apply the 15 non-negotiable rules above to the generated HTML/CSS.
5. Emit set_form_meta with customHtml + customCss (or customHtmlAppend/customCssAppend for additive tweaks).
entries.Title: AI Design Master Prompt (15 non-negotiable rules + creative direction)

entries.Summary: 8 archetype regex patterns + 8 field-group classifications + 8 derived layout hints. Use these when the user asks for design help without a clear style name.
entries.Body:
FORM ARCHETYPE DETECTION (run on title + description + flattened field labels/types as a lowercased haystack):

| Archetype          | Regex (matches any)                                                            |
|--------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| healthcare         | /patient|medical|health|clinic|doctor|insurance|symptom|allerg/                |
| event              | /event|ticket|attendee|conference|seminar|workshop|rsvp/                       |
| job application    | /job|career|resume|cv|employment|position|applicant/                           |
| booking            | /booking|reservation|schedule|appointment|check.?in|check.?out|travel/         |
| feedback           | /feedback|review|rating|survey|satisfaction/                                   |
| contact            | /contact|lead|quote|enquiry|inquiry|message/                                   |
| payment            | /payment|billing|checkout|donation|purchase|order/                             |
| (fallback)         | professional intake                                                            |

Order matters â€” match the FIRST hit top-to-bottom. The detected archetype goes into the master prompt as "Form archetype: <archetype>." line.

FIELD-GROUP CLASSIFICATION (per field, by lowercased key + label + type):

| Group     | Regex (key + label, OR type contains)                                                  | Layout treatment                              |
|-----------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------|
| name      | /first.?name|last.?name|full.?name|name/                                               | Row [6,6] when â‰¥ 2 matches                    |
| contact   | /email|phone|mobile|contact|website|company/                                           | 2-column row when â‰¥ 2 matches                 |
| dates     | /date|birth|dob|time/ OR type contains date/time                                       | Compact row alignment                         |
| choices   | type radio|dropdown|select|checkbox                                                    | Breathing room, clear selection styling       |
| longText  | type textarea, OR label/key contains paragraph|message|notes|comment|description       | FULL WIDTH near bottom                        |
| uploads   | type contains upload|file|image|signature                                              | FULL WIDTH, visually distinct                 |
| profile   | /gender|city|state|country|address|zip|postal|age|department|role/                     | Short rows for rhythm                         |
| other     | (anything not above)                                                                   | Normal flow                                   |

LAYOUT HINTS DERIVED:
- â‰¥ 2 name fields â†’ "Put name fields on the same row when space allows: â€¦"
- â‰¥ 2 contact fields â†’ "Balanced 2-column row â€¦"
- â‰¥ 2 profile fields â†’ "Group compact profile fields into short rows for rhythm â€¦"
- â‰¥ 1 dates field â†’ "Date/time fields should align neatly with other compact fields â€¦"
- â‰¥ 1 choices â†’ "Choice fields should have breathing room and clear selection styling â€¦"
- â‰¥ 1 longText â†’ "Long text fields should span full width near the bottom â€¦"
- â‰¥ 1 uploads â†’ "Upload/signature widgets should be full width and visually distinct â€¦"
- (none of the above) â†’ "Use a clean 1-2 column responsive layout with strong spacing rhythm."

Hidden + Html + Row fields are EXCLUDED from the flattened data field list (they are structural/utility). Section fields are kept (so their headings appear in the generated layout).
entries.Title: Form archetype detection + field-group layout heuristics

entries.Summary: Only 12 themes ship CSS on disk. Any other name silently breaks input styling because host site CSS (DNN, Oqtane, Bootstrap) bleeds through. Dispatcher rejects unknown values with [THEME-001].
entries.Body:
The MegaForm renderer ships exactly 12 themed CSS classes in megaform-themes.css. Any other value silently breaks the form — the host site (DNN / Oqtane / Bootstrap / Tailwind / Material) bleeds through and inputs render with no border + collapsed vertical padding because the host resets `input { padding: 0 14px }`.

VALID THEME NAMES (only these 12, plus the literal "custom"):

1. default          — Indigo accent, white card, standard spacing.
2. minimal          — Ultra-light, mostly whitespace, hairline borders.
3. modern-blue      — Sapphire/azure palette, modern SaaS feel.
4. warm-sunset      — Orange/coral gradients, friendly.
5. dark-elegance    — #0f172a background, refined dark.
6. nature-green     — Sage/forest, organic feel.
7. flat-material    — Material Design, layered shadow.
8. classic-formal   — Serif headings, black/gray palette.
9. playful          — Bright colors, rounded corners.
10. healthcare      — Teal+white, clinical clarity.
11. executive       — Charcoal+gold luxury.
12. tech-startup    — Bold accent, geometric.

PLUS the literal "custom" — only valid when paired with customHtml + customCss that scope EVERYTHING to .mfp (see form_pattern-ai-design-master-prompt).

RULES:
- Never invent theme names (e.g. "pure-grid-premium", "modern-elegant", "italian-luxury"). The dispatcher rejects unknown values with [THEME-001].
- If you want a custom design, set theme:"custom" + emit BOTH customHtml AND customCss. Empty/truncated customCss with theme:"custom" makes the form unstyled because the .mfp wrapper has no styling rules.
- For SHORT styling tweaks on an existing themed form, prefer customCssAppend over replacing the theme.
- When uncertain, pick "default" — it always renders with a clean card.
entries.Title: Valid theme names (12 themes + custom) — never hallucinate

entries.Summary: Top-level guide to MegaForm rules: 14 operators, 8 actions, WHEN/THEN/ELSE, priority, the rule JSON shape, dispatcher contract (writes to 3 slots), canonical ID conventions, Visual QA checklist. v28+ validates shape via validateRuleArray.
entries.Body:
MegaForm has a runtime rule engine that mutates the form DOM whenever any input fires `change` or `input`.

âš¡ DISPATCHER CONTRACT (v20260530-28): rules are committed via the AI dispatcher in TWO ways. The `validateRuleArray` helper checks shape BEFORE write â€” invalid shapes get [RULES-001] reject so half-broken rules never reach the schema.

(1) ADD/REPLACE rules on existing form (most common):
```json
{ "op":"set_form_meta", "rules":[ {RuleDefinition}, ... ] }
```
The dispatcher writes to ALL THREE storage slots (schema.settings.rules, schema.rules, schema.rulesJson) so every loader (rule-builder-ui, renderer, save-as-template) sees them. The Rules tab repaints automatically.

(2) APPEND rules:
```json
{ "op":"set_form_meta", "rulesAppend":[ {RuleDefinition} ] }
```

(3) When emitting `replace_form_schema`, put rules INSIDE the schema settings (canonical) OR at top-level (auto-lifted):
```json
{ "op":"replace_form_schema", "preserveCustomizations":true,
  "schema":{ "version":"1.0", "fields":[...],
             "settings":{ "rules":[ {RuleDefinition}, ... ] } } }
```

ðŸ›‘ COMMON FAILURE: emitting `{"rules":[...]}` AT THE TOP LEVEL of set_form_meta WITHOUT going through validateRuleArray fails because pre-v28 dispatcher silently dropped the key. Form 281 ("ÄÄƒng kÃ½ ná»™i trÃº sinh viÃªn") showed `rules:[]` despite the AI claiming success. Always use v28+ which writes to all 3 locations and validates shape.

CANONICAL RULE DEFINITION SHAPE (paste verbatim, swap field keys + values):
```json
{
  "id": "definition_show_step2",
  "name": "Step 2 visibility â€” when gender=Male",
  "enabled": true,
  "priority": 1,
  "when": {
    "id":   "group_a",
    "type": "group",
    "logic":"all",
    "children": [
      {
        "id":"rule_a", "type":"rule",
        "field":"gender", "operator":"eq", "value":"male"
      }
    ]
  },
  "then": [
    { "id":"ta_male_section",   "action":"show",    "targetType":"section", "target":"sec_male_only" },
    { "id":"ta_required_male",  "action":"require", "targetType":"field",   "target":"male_specific_field" }
  ],
  "else": [
    { "id":"te_male_section",   "action":"hide",     "targetType":"section", "target":"sec_male_only" },
    { "id":"te_optional_male",  "action":"optional", "targetType":"field",   "target":"male_specific_field" }
  ]
}
```

ID CONVENTIONS (matching production templates in MEGAFORM TEMPLATES/DefaultTemplates - Deployed/rules-forms):
- Rule Definition: `definition_<purpose>`
- Group: `group_<letter>`
- Leaf condition: `rule_<letter>`
- THEN action: `ta_<target>`
- ELSE action: `te_<target>`

The dispatcher's validator auto-fills missing ids/name/enabled/priority â€” but production templates always carry them explicitly. AI should follow the same convention.

VALIDATOR ENFORCES:
- `when.type` must be `"group"` or `"rule"`. `group` has `logic:"all"|"any"` + `children:[node...]` (non-empty). `rule` has `field, operator, value`.
- `then`/`else` are arrays. At least ONE must be non-empty.
- Each action needs `action`, `targetType` (`"field"|"section"|"step"`), `target` (key). `setValue` needs `value`.

14 operators (case-insensitive, numeric coerce):
`eq, neq, gt, gte, lt, lte, contains, startsWith, endsWith, in, notIn, isEmpty, isNotEmpty, isTrue, isFalse`

For `in` / `notIn`: comma-separate values: `"value":"A,B,C"`.
For `isEmpty/isNotEmpty/isTrue/isFalse`: no `value` field needed.

8 actions:
`show, hide, require, optional, enable, disable, setValue, clear`

3 target types: `field, section, step`.

VISUAL QA CHECKLIST after emitting rules:
1. Open the Rules tab on the form builder. Each rule should appear in the left sidebar with ON badge + priority number.
2. Click each rule â€” the right pane shows WHEN/THEN/ELSE editor populated with your conditions/actions.
3. Open the form view (/xx?formid=N). Change the trigger field â€” the target should show/hide/require/etc. in real time.
4. If nothing happens: check trigger field key matches `when.field` exactly (case-sensitive, snake_case).
5. If the target stays visible after the condition reverses: you forgot the `else` branch with the inverse action.

WHAT RULES CAN NOT DO (use workarounds):
- Auto-CALCULATE numbers (no formula action). Use DataGrid with computeFormula (see form_pattern-formula-datagrid).
- No "any field changes" trigger.
- No async/server-side conditions.
entries.Title: Rule system overview (engine, shape, dispatcher contract v28, canonical IDs, visual QA checklist)

entries.Summary: All 14 operators supported by rule-engine.ts with applies-to types + a worked example per operator.
entries.Body:
14 operators are accepted by the rule engine. Each operator goes into the `operator` field of a leaf condition. Comparison is case-insensitive for strings, with numeric coercion when both sides parse as numbers.

| Code        | Label              | Applies to                            | Notes |
|-------------|--------------------|---------------------------------------|-------|
| eq          | equals             | all                                   | Case-insensitive for strings, numeric coerce |
| neq         | not equals         | all                                   | Inverse of eq |
| gt          | > greater than     | number, date                          | parseFloat comparison; dates as ISO strings |
| gte         | >= or equal        | number, date                          | |
| lt          | < less than        | number, date                          | |
| lte         | <= or equal        | number, date                          | |
| contains    | contains           | text, textarea, email, url            | substring, case-insensitive |
| startsWith  | starts with        | text, textarea                        | substring, case-insensitive |
| endsWith    | ends with          | text, textarea                        | substring, case-insensitive |
| in          | is in list         | select, radio, checkbox               | value matches ANY token in comma-separated `value` (e.g. "A,B,C") |
| notIn       | is not in list     | select, radio, checkbox               | inverse of in |
| isEmpty     | is empty           | all                                   | value is null/undefined/"" (no `value` field needed) |
| isNotEmpty  | is not empty       | all                                   | |
| isTrue      | is true            | checkbox                              | value truthy |
| isFalse     | is false           | checkbox                              | value falsy |

Examples:
```json
{ "type":"rule", "field":"age",        "operator":"gte",        "value":"18" }
{ "type":"rule", "field":"country",    "operator":"in",         "value":"VN,TH,SG" }
{ "type":"rule", "field":"comments",   "operator":"contains",   "value":"urgent" }
{ "type":"rule", "field":"agree_tos",  "operator":"isTrue" }
{ "type":"rule", "field":"deposit",    "operator":"isNotEmpty" }
```

Pitfalls:
- For ranges (between 10 and 20), use a GROUP with logic="all" and two leaves (gte 10 + lte 20).
- For exclusion (NOT in any), use logic="all" with neq leaves OR a single notIn.
- isEmpty/isNotEmpty/isTrue/isFalse do NOT need a `value` key â€” the engine ignores it.
entries.Title: 14 condition operators (eq, neq, gt/gte/lt/lte, contains/startsWith/endsWith, in/notIn, isEmpty/isNotEmpty, isTrue/isFalse)

entries.Summary: All 8 actions + 3 target types (field/section/step) + worked examples + the cascade/priority gotcha.
entries.Body:
8 actions are accepted by the rule engine. Each lives in `then` OR `else`. `targetType` is one of `field`, `section`, `step`. `target` is the key (snake_case) of the destination element. The renderer finds it via `data-field-key`, `data-section-key`, `data-step-key`.

| Code     | Effect                                                       | Uses `value`? |
|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|---------------|
| show     | Remove `display:none`                                        | no            |
| hide     | Add `display:none`                                           | no            |
| require  | Add `required` attr + `.mf-required` CSS class               | no            |
| optional | Remove `required` + class                                    | no            |
| enable   | Remove `disabled` attribute                                  | no            |
| disable  | Add `disabled` attribute                                     | no            |
| setValue | Write a string into `value` (and fire input event)           | YES           |
| clear    | Set `value=""` (and fire input event)                        | no            |

Examples (action objects):
```json
{ "action":"show",     "targetType":"section", "target":"sec_veteran_info" }
{ "action":"hide",     "targetType":"field",   "target":"spouse_name" }
{ "action":"require",  "targetType":"field",   "target":"insurance_number" }
{ "action":"optional", "targetType":"field",   "target":"middle_name" }
{ "action":"enable",   "targetType":"field",   "target":"submit_btn_extra" }
{ "action":"disable",  "targetType":"field",   "target":"locked_field" }
{ "action":"setValue", "targetType":"field",   "target":"tax_rate", "value":"0.08" }
{ "action":"clear",    "targetType":"field",   "target":"discount_code" }
```

Auto-revert behavior: when WHEN evaluates true, only THEN runs (and any earlier ELSE for the same rule does NOT auto-undo). For a clean show/hide pair, put `show` in THEN and `hide` in ELSE on the SAME rule (the engine evaluates both branches per rule).

Cascading: rules are sorted by `priority` ascending. If Rule A's THEN sets field X, and Rule B reads field X in its WHEN, set Rule A`s priority lower than Rule B's. The engine does ONE pass per evaluate, so a setValue cascade needs explicit ordering.

Pitfalls:
- Action targets MUST match field key exactly (case-sensitive). AI should grab the canvas snapshot key, NOT the label.
- `require` on a `Section` propagates to all child fields (renderer applies recursively).
- `setValue` on a Select must match an `options[].value`, not the label.
- `setValue` writes a STRING. For checkbox use "true"/"false". For dates use ISO "YYYY-MM-DD".
entries.Title: 8 rule actions (show/hide/require/optional/enable/disable/setValue/clear) â€” incl. cascade ordering

entries.Summary: Build nested condition trees with ALL/ANY logic. Empty groups are FALSE. There is no NOT group â€” use neq/notIn/isEmpty or De Morgan.
entries.Title: Condition groups (ALL/ANY, nested, De Morgan for NOT)

entries.Summary: Conditional section visibility â€” common for veteran info, spouse details, company billing. Includes the ELSE hide pair + in operator for multi-value triggers.
entries.Body:
Use case: hide an entire SECTION until a key field has a specific value. Common patterns:
- "Veteran info" section appears only when employmentType="veteran"
- "Spouse details" section appears only when maritalStatus="married"
- "Company billing" section appears only when accountType="business"

Required setup:
1. Have a Section field in the form with a stable `key` (snake_case).
2. The trigger field (the one whose value gates the section) must exist BEFORE the section.

Rule JSON (paste verbatim, swap keys):
```json
{
  "id": "rule_show_spouse",
  "name": "Show spouse details when married",
  "enabled": true,
  "priority": 1,
  "when": {
    "type": "rule", "field": "marital_status", "operator": "eq", "value": "married"
  },
  "then": [
    { "action": "show", "targetType": "section", "target": "sec_spouse" }
  ],
  "else": [
    { "action": "hide", "targetType": "section", "target": "sec_spouse" }
  ]
}
```

Tips:
- Always put a matching `hide` in `else` â€” otherwise the section stays visible after the user changes the trigger away.
- For multi-value trigger (e.g. show when in ["married","domestic_partnership"]), use `"operator":"in", "value":"married,domestic_partnership"`.
- If you also want the section's child fields to be REQUIRED only when visible, add a parallel rule with action=`require` for each child + ELSE optional. Or target the section with action=`require` (propagates).
entries.Title: Show/Hide a section based on a trigger field

entries.Summary: Conditional require/optional â€” pair both actions so the field flips back when the trigger reverses, otherwise the form locks up.
entries.Body:
Use case: make a field required only when another field has a value. Examples:
- `insurance_number` required only when has_insurance=true
- `other_specify` required only when reason="Other"
- `move_in_date` required only when housing_preference != "Not interested"

Rule JSON:
```json
{
  "id": "rule_require_other_specify",
  "name": "Require other_specify when reason=Other",
  "enabled": true,
  "priority": 1,
  "when": { "type": "rule", "field": "reason", "operator": "eq", "value": "Other" },
  "then": [ { "action": "require",  "targetType": "field", "target": "other_specify" } ],
  "else": [ { "action": "optional", "targetType": "field", "target": "other_specify" } ]
}
```

Always pair `require`/`optional` so the field flips back when the condition turns false. Otherwise the field stays required forever and blocks submit.

For form-level required propagation: target a Section with `require` and every child input gets required=true (renderer cascades).
entries.Title: Make a field required only when another field meets a condition

entries.Summary: setValue writes a static string. Cascade via priority ordering. Cannot do arithmetic â€” for that use DataGrid formula engine.
entries.Body:
`setValue` writes a STATIC string into the target field. The runtime fires `input` events after the write, so subsequent rules (with HIGHER priority numbers) get a chance to react.

Use cases:
- Auto-set `tax_rate` to "0.08" when country="US", "0.20" when country="GB".
- Auto-set `currency_code` based on country.
- Pre-fill `delivery_method` to "express" when product_type="perishable".

Single-trigger example (country=US â†’ tax_rate=0.08):
```json
{
  "id": "rule_us_tax",
  "name": "US tax = 8%",
  "enabled": true,
  "priority": 1,
  "when": { "type":"rule", "field":"country", "operator":"eq", "value":"US" },
  "then": [ { "action":"setValue", "targetType":"field", "target":"tax_rate", "value":"0.08" } ],
  "else": []
}
```

Multi-branch (use multiple rules with the SAME priority OR mutually-exclusive WHEN):
```json
[
  { "id":"r_us", "priority":1, "when":{"type":"rule","field":"country","operator":"eq","value":"US"}, "then":[{"action":"setValue","targetType":"field","target":"tax_rate","value":"0.08"}], "else":[] },
  { "id":"r_gb", "priority":1, "when":{"type":"rule","field":"country","operator":"eq","value":"GB"}, "then":[{"action":"setValue","targetType":"field","target":"tax_rate","value":"0.20"}], "else":[] },
  { "id":"r_vn", "priority":1, "when":{"type":"rule","field":"country","operator":"eq","value":"VN"}, "then":[{"action":"setValue","targetType":"field","target":"tax_rate","value":"0.10"}], "else":[] }
]
```

Cascade (setValue triggers a downstream rule):
- Rule A: country=US â†’ setValue tax_rate=0.08 (priority 1)
- Rule B: tax_rate >= 0.10 â†’ show "high tax warning" message (priority 2)
Order priorities so A runs first.

KEY GAP: setValue can NOT compute (e.g. setValue total=subtotal+tax is impossible directly). For real math see form_pattern-formula-datagrid + form_pattern-money-calculation-pattern.
entries.Title: SetValue + cascading rules (auto-fill fields based on conditions)

entries.Summary: The ONLY built-in calc engine. Per-row computeFormula + aggregate totalFormula + totalField writeback to a top-level form input. Covers invoices, orders, time sheets.
entries.Body:
The DataGrid widget has a real client-side formula evaluator (megaform-widget-datagrid.ts:154 `evalLocal`). It is the ONLY built-in way to auto-calculate inside a MegaForm.

Supported functions (case-sensitive, namespaced):
- Math.Round(x, decimals), Math.Min(a,b), Math.Max(a,b), Math.Abs(x), Math.Floor(x), Math.Ceiling(x)
- Round(x,d), Abs(x), Floor(x), Ceiling(x)
- If(cond, a, b) â€” ternary
- Sum("expr"), Avg("expr"), Min("expr"), Max("expr") â€” AGGREGATES across all rows. Argument must be a QUOTED string expression evaluated per-row.

Per-row computed column shape:
```json
{ "key":"line_total", "label":"Total", "type":"computed",
  "width":"120px", "decimals":2, "computeFormula":"qty * price" }
```

Per-grid total (writes into top-level form field):
```json
{
  "type":"DataGrid",
  "key":"line_items",
  "label":"Line items",
  "widgetProps":{
    "columns":[
      { "key":"desc",  "label":"Item",     "type":"text",     "width":"40%" },
      { "key":"qty",   "label":"Qty",      "type":"number",   "width":"15%", "decimals":0 },
      { "key":"price", "label":"Unit",     "type":"currency", "width":"20%", "decimals":2 },
      { "key":"line",  "label":"Subtotal", "type":"computed", "width":"25%", "decimals":2,
        "computeFormula":"qty * price" }
    ],
    "totalFormula":"Sum(\"qty * price\")",
    "totalField":"grand_total"
  }
}
```

How `totalField` reaches a top-level form input:
- After computing `totalFormula`, DataGrid sets the value of the form field whose key matches `totalField` (`grand_total` here). The field must already exist in `schema.fields` (typically as a Number or Currency input, often `readOnly:true` so users don't override the computed total).

Per-row recompute timing: every cell `input`/`change` event triggers `recomputeAll()` â€” all computed cells re-evaluate, then `totalFormula` recomputes, then totalField gets the new sum.

Formatting:
- `type:"currency"` adds a currency symbol per the form locale.
- `decimals` controls displayed precision (raw value is full-precision).
- For non-USD currency (VND, EUR), the column declares `decimals:0` for VND.

GAPS:
- No cross-DataGrid math.
- No formula in a plain Number field (must live inside a DataGrid column).
- For non-line-item calculation (e.g. "deposit = subtotal * 0.2" displayed as standalone field), put deposit as a DataGrid column with `computeFormula:"Sum(\"qty*price\") * 0.2"` â€” but that re-runs Sum per row, which is wasteful. Prefer a single read-only computed column at the bottom.
entries.Title: Auto-calculate with DataGrid (Sum/Avg/Min/Max/If + per-row computeFormula)

entries.Summary: End-to-end form pattern with DataGrid line-items + per-country tax rule prefill + grand-total writeback. Includes the tax/grand-total gap and 3 workarounds.
entries.Body:
Canonical pattern when the user says "I want a form that auto-calculates total / tax / discount / grand total":

STEP 1 â€” Add a `DataGrid` line-items widget with computed Subtotal column + totalFormula.
STEP 2 â€” Add a `Number` field `grand_total` (readOnly:true) that the DataGrid will write into.
STEP 3 â€” (Optional) For tax / discount, add Number fields `tax_rate` (default 0.08) and `discount_amount`. The DataGrid can include a `tax_line` and `discount_line` virtual column that pulls those values, but the simpler approach is to keep DataGrid pure line-items and use a final `final_total` columns OR a final read-only field with `setValue` rules.
STEP 4 â€” Add Rules for prefilling tax_rate based on country (see form_pattern-rules-setvalue-cascade).

Canonical schema fragment:
```json
{ "version":"1.0", "fields":[
  { "key":"customer_name", "type":"Text", "label":"Customer", "required":true },
  { "key":"country", "type":"Select", "label":"Country", "options":[
    {"value":"US","label":"United States"},{"value":"GB","label":"United Kingdom"},{"value":"VN","label":"Vietnam"}
  ]},
  { "key":"tax_rate", "type":"Number", "label":"Tax rate", "readOnly":true, "defaultValue":"0.08" },

  { "key":"line_items", "type":"DataGrid", "label":"Line items",
    "widgetProps":{
      "columns":[
        { "key":"desc",  "label":"Item",     "type":"text",     "width":"40%" },
        { "key":"qty",   "label":"Qty",      "type":"number",   "width":"15%", "decimals":0 },
        { "key":"price", "label":"Unit",     "type":"currency", "width":"20%", "decimals":2 },
        { "key":"line",  "label":"Subtotal", "type":"computed", "width":"25%", "decimals":2,
          "computeFormula":"qty * price" }
      ],
      "totalFormula":"Sum(\"qty * price\")",
      "totalField":"subtotal",
      "minRows":1
    }
  },

  { "key":"subtotal",   "type":"Number", "label":"Subtotal",     "readOnly":true, "defaultValue":"0" },
  { "key":"tax_amount", "type":"Number", "label":"Tax",          "readOnly":true, "defaultValue":"0" },
  { "key":"grand_total","type":"Number", "label":"Grand total",  "readOnly":true, "defaultValue":"0" }
],
"settings":{
  "rules":[
    { "id":"r_us", "priority":1, "enabled":true,
      "when":{"type":"rule","field":"country","operator":"eq","value":"US"},
      "then":[{"action":"setValue","targetType":"field","target":"tax_rate","value":"0.08"}], "else":[] },
    { "id":"r_gb", "priority":1, "enabled":true,
      "when":{"type":"rule","field":"country","operator":"eq","value":"GB"},
      "then":[{"action":"setValue","targetType":"field","target":"tax_rate","value":"0.20"}], "else":[] },
    { "id":"r_vn", "priority":1, "enabled":true,
      "when":{"type":"rule","field":"country","operator":"eq","value":"VN"},
      "then":[{"action":"setValue","targetType":"field","target":"tax_rate","value":"0.10"}], "else":[] }
  ]
}}
```

GAP: `subtotal * tax_rate = tax_amount` and `subtotal + tax_amount = grand_total` cannot be computed by rules alone. Two ways to close it:
1. Add a hidden DataGrid row whose `computeFormula` does `Sum("qty * price") * <field:tax_rate>` â€” feasible but ugly.
2. Use a single `final_total` column with `computeFormula:"qty * price * (1 + <tax_rate_field>)"`. The formula engine does NOT support reading top-level form fields (only row cells + aggregates). So this requires injecting the tax rate AS a column, not as a separate top-level field.

The cleanest live MegaForm pattern today: keep the DataGrid the source-of-truth for the WHOLE total, including tax. Add a column `tax_line` whose `computeFormula` is `qty * price * 0.08` (or a column constant per row). The totalField then receives a pre-tax-included grand total. The user does not see tax separately, which is sometimes acceptable.

For a TRUE separated subtotal/tax/grand_total display, ship a tiny script token in `customScripts` listening to `input` events on the DataGrid total field + tax_rate field, computing tax_amount + grand_total and writing them. See form_pattern-script-token-money-calc.
entries.Title: Money auto-calculation canonical pattern (invoice/order/quote)

entries.Summary: Workaround for the rule engine no-formula gap: a tiny customScripts JS that listens to subtotal+tax_rate and writes tax_amount+grand_total. Requires customHtml/customScripts mode.
entries.Title: Script-token money calc (separated subtotal/tax/grand_total live updates)

entries.Summary: Concrete worked example: 4 fields, 3 chained rules covering show/hide + require/optional + setValue with proper priority ordering.
entries.Body:
A concrete worked example â€” the "Form co hearing" the user built. Fields:
- gender (Dropdown: Male/Female)
- housing_preference (Dropdown: Single Room / Shared / Family Apartment / Not interested)
- move_in_date (Date)
- special_needs (Long Text)

Desired behavior:
- Move-in date appears only when housing_preference is NOT "Not interested".
- Special needs becomes REQUIRED when housing_preference = "Family Apartment" (because we need to know about children/elderly).
- For gender=Female + housing_preference=Shared, auto-set special_needs placeholder to suggest mentioning women-only rooming preference.

Rules JSON (paste into schema.settings.rules):
```json
[
  {
    "id":"r_move_in_visibility", "name":"Show move-in date unless not interested", "enabled":true, "priority":1,
    "when":{ "type":"rule","field":"housing_preference","operator":"neq","value":"Not interested" },
    "then":[ {"action":"show","targetType":"field","target":"move_in_date"} ],
    "else":[ {"action":"hide","targetType":"field","target":"move_in_date"} ]
  },
  {
    "id":"r_family_needs_required", "name":"Family apartment â†’ special needs required", "enabled":true, "priority":2,
    "when":{ "type":"rule","field":"housing_preference","operator":"eq","value":"Family Apartment" },
    "then":[ {"action":"require","targetType":"field","target":"special_needs"} ],
    "else":[ {"action":"optional","targetType":"field","target":"special_needs"} ]
  },
  {
    "id":"r_female_shared_hint", "name":"Female + Shared â†’ suggest preference", "enabled":true, "priority":3,
    "when":{ "type":"group", "logic":"all", "children":[
      {"type":"rule","field":"gender","operator":"eq","value":"Female"},
      {"type":"rule","field":"housing_preference","operator":"eq","value":"Shared"}
    ]},
    "then":[ {"action":"setValue","targetType":"field","target":"special_needs","value":"Please mention if you prefer a women-only room."} ],
    "else":[]
  }
]
```

Priority ordering: 1 (visibility) â†’ 2 (validation toggle) â†’ 3 (setValue hint). This ensures the field is visible & required before the hint setValue runs.
entries.Title: End-to-end conditional housing form (show/require/setValue mixed)

entries.Summary: Skip an entire wizard page using show/hide on targetType="step". Trigger field must live on an earlier step.
entries.Body:
In multi-page forms (settings.multiPage=true), each page is a `step`. Rules can hide an entire step so the wizard skips it.

Setup:
1. settings.multiPage = true.
2. Each page has a step key (auto-assigned `step_1`, `step_2`, etc., or explicit).
3. The rule targets `targetType:"step"` with the step key.

Use case: "Insurance details" step appears only when has_insurance=true.

Rule JSON:
```json
{
  "id":"r_skip_insurance_step", "name":"Skip insurance step if no insurance", "enabled":true, "priority":1,
  "when":{ "type":"rule","field":"has_insurance","operator":"isFalse" },
  "then":[ {"action":"hide","targetType":"step","target":"step_3_insurance"} ],
  "else":[ {"action":"show","targetType":"step","target":"step_3_insurance"} ]
}
```

The wizard navigation auto-respects hidden steps â€” Next button jumps to the next visible step.

Gotcha: the trigger field must be on an EARLIER step than the one being skipped. Putting `has_insurance` on step 5 and trying to skip step 3 leaves step 3 always visible because the rule fires too late.
entries.Title: Conditional wizard steps (skip a page based on a field)

entries.Summary: Consolidated cascade pattern: 2-level / 3-level / pickerâ†’detail chains, optionsSql shape, dispatcher guards, snake_case â†’ camelCase placeholder rule.
entries.Body:
Cascade dropdown pattern: a child Select / DataRepeater / DataGrid is filtered by the value(s) selected in one or more PARENT fields. The relationship lives in widgetProps via `optionsDependsOn` (for Select / Radio / Checkbox) or `queryDependsOn` (for DataGrid / DataRepeater / DynamicLabel).

KEY MECHANICS:
- Parent field: must have its OWN options source (static `options` array OR `properties.optionsSql`) so the dropdown actually has values to pick.
- Child field: declares the parent key(s) it depends on.
- SQL placeholder: parent key in camelCase prefixed with `:`. Example â€” parent key `country_id` â†’ SQL placeholder `:countryId`. The renderer auto-maps snake_case â†’ camelCase parameter binding.
- Refresh trigger: child auto-refetches whenever ANY of its parents changes.
- Reset on parent change: child value clears so the user can pick a fresh value from the new option set.

CHILD FIELD SHAPES BY WIDGET:

(a) Select / Radio / Checkbox depending on a parent:
```json
{
  "key":"city",
  "type":"Select",
  "label":"City",
  "properties":{
    "optionsSource":"sql",
    "optionsConnectionKey":"DashboardDatabase",
    "optionsSql":"SELECT Id AS value, Name AS label FROM Cities WHERE CountryId = :countryId ORDER BY Name",
    "optionsDependsOn":["country_id"],
    "optionsReloadOnChange":true
  }
}
```

(b) DataRepeater / DataGrid / DynamicLabel filtered by parents:
```json
{
  "key":"scorecard_grid",
  "type":"DataGrid",
  "label":"Scorecards",
  "widgetProps":{
    "useSql":true,
    "dataSource":"sql",
    "connectionKey":"DashboardDatabase",
    "masterQuery":"SELECT * FROM Scorecards WHERE PlayerId = :playerId AND RoundId = :roundId",
    "queryDependsOn":["player_id","round_id"],
    "pageSize":50
  }
}
```

CANONICAL CHAINS:
- 2-level: Country â†’ City. Parent Select with optionsSql for countries; child Select with optionsDependsOn:["country_id"].
- 3-level: Country â†’ Province â†’ City. Each level's optionsDependsOn lists ALL upstream parents (not just the immediate parent) so the cascade resets cleanly: country reloads provinces, province reloads cities â€” and changing country auto-clears province AND city.
- Picker â†’ Detail: Select PARENT â†’ Select CHILD â†’ DataRepeater DETAIL. The user PICKS (Selects) before the DataRepeater shows. Never make a DataRepeater an intermediate picker (DataRepeater is display-only â€” see widget-datarepeater).

DISPATCHER GUARDS (dispatcher rules in MF_AI_KB_Rules):
- DR-001 â€” DataRepeater missing masterQuery is rejected (would render forever-loading spinner).
- DL-001 â€” DynamicLabel missing useSql:true OR resultMode is rejected.
- Cascade parent missing options/optionsSql is rejected (parent would never have a value to cascade).

KEY VS CAMEL CASE BUG: write SQL placeholder in camelCase (`:countryId`) even though the field key is snake_case (`country_id`). The renderer normalizes. Writing `:country_id` in SQL works in some queries but fails when the connection driver strips underscores. Always camelCase.

VERIFICATION BEFORE EMITTING SQL: call `get_table_columns(tableName)` on EVERY table referenced in the SQL â€” never assume FK columns exist by name. Tables called Players / Rounds / Scorecards usually have FK columns in the SCORECARDS table, not the lookup tables.

SEE ALSO:
- cascade-2level â€” 2-level countryâ†’city template
- cascade-3level â€” countryâ†’provinceâ†’city
- widget-datarepeater-cascade â€” DataRepeater binding to master query
entries.Title: Cascade SQL dropdown (parent â†’ child filtering via optionsDependsOn / queryDependsOn)

entries.Summary: Canonical Jotform-style premium 2-column split with image hero on the left and form fields on the right. Every field MUST appear as {{field:KEY}} in customHtml. Full CSS template + responsive collapse to single column.
entries.Body:
Jotform-style premium 2-column split layout: image / branded hero on the LEFT (~40-50% width with title + subtitle overlay), form fields on the RIGHT. The most-requested "premium" look users ask for in the AI Form Creator modal. ðŸ›‘ MANDATORY RULE â€” every field in `fields[]` MUST appear as `{{field:KEY}}` somewhere in customHtml. If you list 5 fields but only place 3 placeholders, 2 fields render INVISIBLE at runtime (the previous AI Form Creator bug â€” form 281 showed right column blank because AI emitted customHtml without placeholders). Validator [PRESERVE-001] / [SPLIT-001] rejects this. Canonical schema (copy-paste + swap field labels/keys): ```json { "version":"1.0", "title":"ÄÄƒng kÃ½ Workshop", "fields":[ { "key":"row_name", "type":"Row", "columns":[ { "span":6, "fields":[{ "key":"first_name", "type":"Text", "label":"First name", "required":true, "placeholder":"Nguyá»…n" }] }, { "span":6, "fields":[{ "key":"last_name", "type":"Text", "label":"Last name", "required":true, "placeholder":"VÄƒn A" }] } ]}, { "key":"email", "type":"Email", "label":"Email", "required":true, "placeholder":"ban@example.com" }, { "key":"phone", "type":"CompositePhone", "label":"Phone", "required":true }, { "key":"start_date","type":"Date", "label":"Start date", "required":true }, { "key":"session", "type":"Radio", "label":"Session", "required":true, "options":[{"value":"morning","label":"Morning"},{"value":"evening","label":"Evening"}], "properties":{"optionColumns":2} }, { "key":"notes", "type":"Textarea", "label":"Notes" } ], "settings":{ "theme":"custom", "submitButtonText":"ÄÄƒng kÃ½", "customHtml":"

{{form:title}}

{{form:description}}

{{field:row_name}}{{field:email}}{{field:phone}}{{field:start_date}}{{field:session}}{{field:notes}}
{{form:submit}}
", "customCss":".mfp.mfp-split{display:grid;grid-template-columns:42% 58%;gap:0;min-height:560px;border-radius:16px;overflow:hidden;box-shadow:0 24px 60px -10px rgba(15,23,42,0.18);background:#fff;font-family:'Inter',-apple-system,sans-serif;max-width:1100px;margin:24px auto}.mfp.mfp-split .mfp-hero{position:relative;overflow:hidden}.mfp.mfp-split .mfp-hero-img{width:100%;height:100%;object-fit:cover;display:block;filter:brightness(0.7)}.mfp.mfp-split .mfp-hero-overlay{position:absolute;inset:0;display:flex;flex-direction:column;justify-content:center;padding:36px 40px;color:#fff;background:linear-gradient(135deg,rgba(15,23,42,0.55) 0%,rgba(15,23,42,0.15) 100%)}.mfp.mfp-split .mfp-hero-title{font-size:36px;font-weight:800;margin:0 0 12px;line-height:1.15;letter-spacing:-0.01em}.mfp.mfp-split .mfp-hero-sub{font-size:15px;opacity:0.92;margin:0;font-weight:400;line-height:1.5}.mfp.mfp-split .mfp-form{padding:48px 44px;display:flex;align-items:center;background:#fafafa}.mfp.mfp-split .mfp-form-inner{width:100%;max-width:440px;margin:0 auto;display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:18px}.mfp.mfp-split .mfp-submit-wrap{margin-top:8px}.mfp.mfp-split input,.mfp.mfp-split textarea,.mfp.mfp-split select{width:100%;border:1px solid #e2e8f0;border-radius:8px;padding:11px 14px;font-size:14px;background:#fff;transition:border-color 0.15s,box-shadow 0.15s;box-sizing:border-box}.mfp.mfp-split input:focus,.mfp.mfp-split textarea:focus,.mfp.mfp-split select:focus{border-color:#6366f1;box-shadow:0 0 0 3px rgba(99,102,241,0.12);outline:none}.mfp.mfp-split .mf-field-label{font-size:13px;font-weight:600;color:#334155;margin-bottom:6px;display:block}.mfp.mfp-split .mf-required{color:#ef4444}.mfp.mfp-split .mf-btn-submit,.mfp.mfp-split button[type=submit]{width:100%;background:linear-gradient(135deg,#6366f1,#8b5cf6);color:#fff;border:0;padding:12px 24px;border-radius:8px;font-weight:600;font-size:14px;cursor:pointer;transition:transform 0.12s,box-shadow 0.15s}.mfp.mfp-split .mf-btn-submit:hover{transform:translateY(-1px);box-shadow:0 8px 16px -4px rgba(99,102,241,0.4)}@media (max-width:760px){.mfp.mfp-split{grid-template-columns:1fr;max-width:600px}.mfp.mfp-split .mfp-hero{min-height:240px}.mfp.mfp-split .mfp-hero-title{font-size:24px}.mfp.mfp-split .mfp-form{padding:28px 24px}}" } } ``` KEY ANATOMY: - `theme:"custom"` â€” required to opt into custom shell. Anything else falls back to themed CSS. - `customHtml` root wrapper: `
â€¦
` â€” the `.mfp` class is required for scoping, the `.mfp-split` is the form-specific class that scoped CSS targets. - Two `
` siblings: `
` (left) and `
` (right). - Hero overlay: `{{form:title}}` and `{{form:description}}` tokens render the form-level title and description â€” the user can edit them in the Builder's Settings tab WITHOUT touching customHtml. - Form column: place EVERY `{{field:KEY}}` placeholder for fields in `fields[]`. If a field is missing from customHtml, that field is invisible at runtime. The order of placeholders controls the rendered order â€” Row fields can stay in any place; they render their internal grid intact. - Submit: `{{form:submit}}` renders the submit button. Wrap it in `.mfp-submit-wrap` so CSS can target the spacing. CSS SCOPING (per the master prompt): - Every rule starts with `.mfp.mfp-split` so it never bleeds into the host site theme or other forms. - Inputs / textareas / selects styled at the `.mfp.mfp-split input` level (NOT a global `input {}` rule â€” that would leak). - `@media (max-width:760px)` collapses to single column on mobile. - `.mf-field-label` and `.mf-required` are the labels the renderer wraps around inputs â€” style them within the `.mfp.mfp-split` scope. IMAGE SOURCE: - Use `https://picsum.photos/seed///` â€” deterministic, no API key, always loads. - NEVER use `images.unsplash.com/photo-` (AI hallucinates 32-char IDs that 404). Validator [IMG-001] rejects. - For local-asset case: `/Portals/0/MyImages/hero.jpg` or `~/Modules/MegaForm/img/hero.jpg` are OK if pre-uploaded. VARIATIONS (swap-in for different vibes): - Image on RIGHT instead of left: change `grid-template-columns:58% 42%` and swap HTML order so `.mfp-form` comes first. - Pull-quote instead of image: replace `` with `
â€¦
` styled with serif font + large drop quote. - Gradient hero (no image): `
` + CSS gradient background, no `` element. WHEN NOT TO USE: - Standard intake / contact / survey forms â€” use a plain Section + Row layout with `theme:"default"` instead. The Jotform-style split is OVERKILL for a 3-field contact form and clutters mobile. - Forms with many sections (â‰¥3) â€” premium hero shells work best with 5-12 fields in ONE conceptual group. - Multi-page wizards (settings.multiPage:true) â€” the wizard navigation buttons fight the .mfp-split grid; use a wizard-themed template instead. VALIDATOR (dispatcher-side): - [PRESERVE-001] â€” every field in `fields[]` whose key is NOT referenced as `{{field:KEY}}` in customHtml triggers a reject. AI must update customHtml when adding fields or drop the field. - [SPLIT-001 future] â€” if grid-template-columns is set, both `.mfp-hero` and `.mfp-form` must be present; otherwise revert to single-column.

entries.Summary: Full-viewport background image with a centered semi-transparent backdrop-blurred form card floating on top. Best for RSVP, invitation, wedding, luxury intake.
entries.Body:
Premium "background image full-page + form floating overlay" layout â€” full viewport background image (often blurred/darkened), centered semi-transparent form card with backdrop-filter blur. Glassmorphism vibe. Best for invitation forms, RSVP, luxury / lifestyle brands. Canonical schema: ```json { "version":"1.0", "title":"You Are Invited", "description":"Save the date â€” June 15, 2026", "fields":[ { "key":"row_name", "type":"Row", "columns":[ { "span":6, "fields":[{ "key":"first_name", "type":"Text", "label":"First name", "required":true }] }, { "span":6, "fields":[{ "key":"last_name", "type":"Text", "label":"Last name", "required":true }] } ]}, { "key":"email", "type":"Email", "label":"Email", "required":true }, { "key":"attending", "type":"Radio", "label":"Will you attend?", "required":true, "options":[{"value":"yes","label":"Yes, I will be there"},{"value":"no","label":"Sorry, can not make it"}], "properties":{"optionColumns":2} }, { "key":"plus_one", "type":"Checkbox", "label":"Bringing a +1" }, { "key":"dietary", "type":"Textarea", "label":"Dietary requirements" } ], "settings":{ "theme":"custom", "submitButtonText":"Send RSVP", "customHtml":"

{{form:title}}

{{form:description}}

{{field:row_name}}{{field:email}}{{field:attending}}{{field:plus_one}}{{field:dietary}}
{{form:submit}}
", "customCss":".mfp.mfp-bg-overlay{position:relative;min-height:100vh;display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;padding:32px 16px;font-family:'Inter',sans-serif}.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay .mfp-bg-img{position:absolute;inset:0;background-image:url('https://picsum.photos/seed/invitation/1920/1200');background-size:cover;background-position:center;filter:brightness(0.55) blur(2px);z-index:0}.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay .mfp-card{position:relative;z-index:1;width:100%;max-width:520px;background:rgba(255,255,255,0.92);backdrop-filter:blur(20px);-webkit-backdrop-filter:blur(20px);border:1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.18);border-radius:18px;padding:40px 36px;box-shadow:0 30px 80px -20px rgba(15,23,42,0.4)}.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay .mfp-card-inner{display:flex;flex-direction:column;gap:16px}.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay .mfp-card-title{font-size:30px;font-weight:800;margin:0 0 4px;color:#0f172a;line-height:1.1;text-align:center}.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay .mfp-card-sub{font-size:14px;color:#475569;margin:0 0 16px;text-align:center}.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay input,.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay textarea,.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay select{width:100%;border:1px solid #e2e8f0;border-radius:8px;padding:11px 14px;font-size:14px;background:#fff;box-sizing:border-box}.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay input:focus,.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay textarea:focus{border-color:#6366f1;box-shadow:0 0 0 3px rgba(99,102,241,0.12);outline:none}.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay .mf-field-label{font-size:13px;font-weight:600;color:#334155;margin-bottom:6px;display:block}.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay .mf-required{color:#ef4444}.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay .mf-btn-submit,.mfp.mfp-bg-overlay button[type=submit]{width:100%;background:linear-gradient(135deg,#6366f1,#8b5cf6);color:#fff;border:0;padding:13px 24px;border-radius:8px;font-weight:600;font-size:14px;cursor:pointer;margin-top:6px}" } } ``` ANATOMY: - `.mfp-bg-img` is ABSOLUTE-positioned with `inset:0` so it covers the full `.mfp-bg-overlay` wrapper. The background-image goes here (not on .mfp itself) so the form card can sit on TOP with z-index:1. - `.mfp-card` has `backdrop-filter:blur(20px)` for the glassmorphism look on browsers that support it. The `-webkit-` prefix covers Safari. - `min-height:100vh` makes the form fill the viewport for that "invitation card floating over photo" vibe. - The background image stays SHARP at center while edges blur via `filter:blur(2px)` â€” feel free to remove the blur for crisp photos. KEYWORDS that trigger this layout: "background image full-page", "áº£nh ná»n cáº£ trang", "invitation", "rsvp", "glassmorphism", "luxury invitation", "wedding RSVP", "elegant", "transparent card", "floating overlay".
entries.Title: Premium full-page background + glassmorphism floating card overlay

entries.Summary: 4 production-ready premium custom-shell layouts with the keyword map AI uses to pick the right one based on the user prompt.
entries.Body:
4 production-ready premium custom-shell layouts. Pick by the user's visual cue (image left/right, image top, full-page background, header band). Each entry has full canonical customHtml + customCss + `{{field:KEY}}` placeholders for all fields â€” paste verbatim, swap field keys, ship. | Slug | Look | When to use | |------|------|-------------| | `form_pattern-premium-split-layout` | Image LEFT (42%) + form RIGHT (58%) | Course signup, workshop, designer-look intake. Jotform-default. | | `form_pattern-premium-hero-top` | Full-width hero image TOP + form BELOW | Webinar/event signup, marketing landing, lead gen. | | `form_pattern-premium-bg-overlay` | Full-page background + centered glass card with fields ON TOP of the background | Invitation, RSVP, luxury / lifestyle, wedding. | | `form_pattern-premium-header-band` | Solid/gradient color HEADER BAND + white form card OVERLAPPING the band edge | Customer support, B2B intake, corporate survey. | All 4 layouts share the same rules: 1. `settings.theme = "custom"` (REQUIRED). 2. customHtml root: `
â€¦
`. 3. ðŸ›‘ every field in `fields[]` MUST appear as `{{field:KEY}}` inside customHtml. Auto-repair in the AI Form Creator will inject missing ones, but AI should emit them correctly first try. 4. Use `{{form:title}}`, `{{form:description}}`, `{{form:submit}}` for editable form-level text. 5. Every CSS rule prefixed by `.mfp.mfp-` â€” never style global tags. Picsum.photos for images. 6. `@media (max-width:640-760px)` collapse to mobile. PICKING THE LAYOUT â€” keyword map: - "split", "2-col", "image left", "image right", "designer" â†’ split-layout - "hero top", "hero banner", "banner trÃªn cÃ¹ng", "landing", "webinar" â†’ hero-top - "background image", "full-page bg", "áº£nh ná»n cáº£ trang", "glassmorphism", "invitation", "rsvp" â†’ bg-overlay - "header band", "header strip", "color header", "corporate", "B2B", "thanh tiÃªu Ä‘á» mÃ u" â†’ header-band - Generic "premium / Jotform / branded" with no specific cue â†’ default to split-layout REUSING ACROSS FORMS: After AI creates one of these forms, the user can switch layouts by changing the wrapper class `.mfp-` AND swapping the customCss block. The customHtml structure for split vs hero-top etc differs (1 vs 2 children of root), so a clean swap requires both.
entries.Title: Premium layouts directory â€” pick split / hero-top / bg-overlay / header-band by keyword
